data.title: Konecranes Secures Order for Seven Hybrid Cranes in Spain
data.description:
Konecranes received an order from CSP Iberian Valencia Terminal to deliver seven hybrid rubber-tired gantry cranes to the Port of Valencia in Spain.

Delivery of the cranes is planned for March 2026, the Finnish crane manufacturer said Friday.

data.title: Innovent Biologics' NDA for Thyroid Eye Disease Drug Gets China Nod
data.description: Innovent Biologics' new drug application (NDA) for teprotumumab N01 injection under the brand name, SYCUME, has received approval from China's National Medical Products Administration for the treatment of thyroid eye disease, a Friday filing with the Hong Kong bourse said.

data.description: Chipotle Mexican Grill has an average rating of overweight and mean price target of $66.57, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Trond Fiskum to take office as Kongsberg Automotive's President & CEO on March 31, 2025
data.description:
Kongsberg Automotive ASA

Kongsberg March 14, 2025: Trond Fiskum, the new President & CEO of Kongsberg Automotive (KA), will assume his role at the company’s headquarters in Kongsberg on March 31, 2025. He was appointed by KA’s Board of Directors on January 29, 2025.

Fiskum has spent a decade (2005-2015) in various leadership positions in KA, and was based both in Norway and Brazil. He led key restructuring and strategy roles, including as a member of the Executive Committee and the Head of Strategy and Business Development. He holds an MSc from NTNU and an MBA from ESADE. Fiskum returns to KA from HMH, a drilling solutions provider, where he served as the Vice President, South America.

Investor relations and media contacts:

Therese Sjöborg Skurdal – Director Group Marketing & Communications

therese.skurdal@ka-group.com

+47 982 14 059

investor.relations@ka-group.com

About Kongsberg Automotive ASA

Kongsberg Automotive provides cutting-edge technology to the global vehicle industry. We drive the global transition to sustainable mobility by putting engineering, sustainability, and innovation into practice. Our product portfolio includes driver and motion control systems, fluid assemblies, and industrial driver interface products. Find more information at: www.kongsbergautomotive.com

https://news.cision.com/kongsberg-automotive-asa/r/trond-fiskum-to-take-office-as-kongsberg-automotive-s-president---ceo-on-march-31--2025%2Cc4119014

Release

data.title: Ethernity Networks to Raise Funds through Share Placing
data.description:
Ethernity Networks launched a share placing to raise gross proceeds of 88,750 pounds sterling, according to a Friday release.

The British data processing semiconductor technology company will place 177,500,000 new ordinary shares for 0.0005 pound each to generate additional working capital and support its creditor settlement plan.

The shares are expected to start trading on London stock exchange's AIM on March 20.

data.title: Britain's Life Science REIT to Begin Strategic Review
data.description:
Life Science REIT plans to commence a strategic review amid headwinds faced by the company since its initial public offering in 2021, according to a Friday release.

The strategic review will explore all options, including the sale of the British real estate investment trust or its portfolio or subsidiaries, changes to its investment strategy and/or management arrangements, and a managed wind-down of the group.

Panmure Liberum will serve as the company's financial adviser to assist with the process.

data.title: Cybozu Profit Rises 43% in 2024
data.description:
Cybozu reported a 43% increase in profit attributable to owners of the parent to 3.56 billion yen for the year ended Dec. 31, 2024 from 2.49 billion yen a year ago, driven by strong cloud service sales.

The software company's earnings basic per share (EPS) rose to 74.99 yen from 52.29 yen a year ago, according to a stock exchange filing on Feb. 13.

Net sales rose nearly 17% to 29.7 billion yen from 25.4 billion yen in the previous year, supported by a growing number of subscriptions for its cloud-based collaboration services.

For year ending Dec. 31, Cybozu expects attributable profit to rise 66% to 5.90 billion yen, or 127.77 yen per share, while net sales are projected to increase 21% to 36.00 billion yen.

The company plans to pay a year-end dividend of 40 yen per share.

data.title: U.K. Manufacturing Weakness Could Continue to Drag on GDP Growth — Market Talk
data.description: The volatility of monthly GDP data means the U.K. economy was likely to struggle to follow the large 0.4% on-month jump in December, Berenberg's Andrew Wishart says. Indeed, on a three-month on three-month basis, GDP climbed 0.2% compared to the 0.1% fall in January alone. Contraction in the manufacturing sector dragged GDP down by 0.1 percentage point, explaining the entire fall. But the industrial struggle was a disappointment given that eurozone industrial output rose in the same month, and PMI surveys suggest this trend could worsen in the U.K., Wishart notes. The export-intensive industrial sector marked a contrast with increasing momentum in services, highlighting a divergence between robust domestic demand but weak exports as the U.K. struggles to compete in international markets, he says. (edward.frankl@wsj.com)

data.title: Stocks rise on China boost, but face weekly dip on trade war concerns
data.description:
Key points:
  • Polish stocks at highest level since 2011
  • Czech stocks hit record highs
  • Mozambique's dollar bond rises after U.S. clears $5 bln LNG loan

Emerging market stocks advanced on Friday, buoyed by heavyweight Chinese shares, but were set for modest weekly declines as U.S. President Donald Trump's tariff threats kept risk-taking in check.

MSCI's index for emerging market stocks CBOE:EFS climbed 0.9% by 0904 GMT, boosted by a more than 2% jump in both Mainland China and Hong Kong .

Despite Friday's gains, the index was on track to fall by more than 1% for the week as jitters over the Trump administration's tariff threats and fears of a U.S. economic slowdown hit risk assets globally.

In its latest trade war volley, the United States on Thursday threatened to slap a 200% tariff on wine, cognac and other alcohol imports from Europe.

U.S. Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer, meanwhile, said on Thursday he would vote to advance a Republican stopgap funding bill, signalling that his party would provide the votes to avert a government shutdown.

Equities in emerging Europe ticked higher, with Poland's blue-chip index hitting its highest level since August 2011, while Czech shares rose almost 1% to an all-time high.

Central and Eastern European currencies were slightly weaker, with Hungary's forint slipping 0.3%.

Emerging market stocks have outperformed the U.S. S&P 500 so far in March, as beaten-down Indian equities have recouped some of their losses and China has vowed more support for its ailing economy.

Central and Eastern Europe were not far behind as hopes of a peace deal between Russia and Ukraine lifted sentiment, in contrast to a sharp drop in American equities, where the S&P 500 slipped into a technical correction.

President Vladimir Putin said on Thursday that Russia supported a U.S. proposal for a ceasefire in Ukraine in principle, but that fighting could not be paused until a number of crucial conditions were worked out or clarified.

"The move lower in U.S. equity markets has been unsettling, and markets are concerned we're heading into a bear market rather than a mere correction," Barclays analysts said in a note.

MSCI's index for emerging market currencies (.MIEM00000CUS) has gained around 1.5% so far this year while the dollar has sagged globally.

A big mover on Friday was Mozambique's sovereign dollar bond (XS2051203946=TE), which gained just under 2 cents, after the United States cleared a long-delayed $5 billion loan for a liquefied natural gas project.

South Africa's rand firmed 0.5% against the dollar after declining earlier this week when Finance Minister Enoch Godongwana presented his revised national budget.

Godongwana will engage with lawmakers about the budget later in the day.

HIGHLIGHTS:

** Polish cenbank's Kotecki sees rate cut proposal by September 'at latest'

** Hungary supports common European defence policy but not joint borrowing, PM Orban says

For TOP NEWS across emerging markets

data.title: Correction: Docusign Fiscal Q4 Earnings, Revenue Rise; Fiscal Q1, 2026 Guidance Set -- Shares Rise
data.description:
(Corrects revenue guidance for fiscal 2026 in the headline and fifth paragraph.)

Docusign late Thursday reported fiscal Q4 non-GAAP net income of $0.86 per diluted share, up from $0.76 a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet, on average, expected $0.85.

Revenue for the quarter ended Jan. 31 was $776.3 million, compared with $712.4 million a year earlier. Analysts expected $761.2 million.

For Q1, the electronic signature company is projecting revenue in a range of $745 million to $749 million. The consensus is $756.8 million.

For fiscal 2026, Docusign said it expects revenue to be $3.13 billion to $3.14 billion. Analysts are looking for $3.15 billion.

Docusign shares recently were up over 10% in recent premarket trading.

The deliverable instrument for all the new derivatives is the stock class

listed at Nasdaq Stockholm in SEK.

Susquehanna International Securities Ltd. (SIG) will be quoting prices for

derivatives on the above-mentioned names.

The series will be included in the Market Notice “New Strikes Stock Products”

that will be sent out after business on Friday, March 21, 2025.

References to Exchange Rules and Regulations of Nasdaq Derivatives Markets

Relevant Contract Specifications for all the new products above are in Chapter

B of the Rules and Regulations for Nasdaq Derivatives Markets.

Please see the Appendixes -section of Rules and Regulations for:

-- Fee Lists;

-- Quotation List (available Contract Terms, Strike Price Intervals, On

Request framework, and further details);

-- Market Model including, i.a. Spread Table, Market Maker Protection

Quantity, Minimum Quote Size - and Order Price Limit tables.

Trading hours and trading calendar are the same as for current Single Stock

Derivatives in thesame underlying markets.

For contact information, please see the attached file.https://view.news.eu.nasdaq.com/view?id=b109c3318e9108137a7040296d5a5ebb5&lang=en&src=omxlink

data.title: Shareholding disclosure - Nordnet AB (publ)
data.description:
On March 14, 2025, Nordnet AB (publ), through its subsidiary NordnetLivsforsikring AS, net bought 456,955 shares in Nordic Financials ASA (ISINNO0012958539). After these transactions, Nordnet AB (publ), through itssubsidiaries, held a total of 1,626,503 shares in the company on aconsolidatedbasis, representing 6.84% of the outstanding shares and votes in the company.

The ownership distribution among Nordnet AB (publ) subsidiaries is as follows: Nordnet Pensionsförsäkring AB: 0.180%Nordnet Livsforsikring AS: 6.656%

Nordnet AB (publ) does not exercise voting rights for these shares. This disclosure is made in accordance with sections 4-2 and 4-3 of theNorwegianSecurities Trading Act and is subject to the disclosure requirement pursuanttosection 5-12 of the same Act.

data.title: Li Auto Profit Drops Despite Record Revenue
data.description:
By Jiahui Huang

Chinese plug-in hybrid specialist Li Auto reported lower net profit for the fourth quarter despite record revenue as a brutal price war hurt its bottom line.

The Beijing-based carmaker said Friday that its net profit fell 38% to 3.52 billion yuan, equivalent to $485.9 million. That beat the 3.22 billion yuan estimate of analysts in a Visible Alpha poll.

Revenue for the period rose 6.1% to 44.27 billion yuan, which the company said was a new quarterly record, topping analysts' estimate of 43.26 billion yuan.

Its gross margin fell to 20.3% from 23.5% a year ago and was lower than 21.5% in the third quarter, it said. The company said the year-over-year margin weakness was due to product-mix changes. It attributed the sequential drop to losses on purchase commitment and lower selling prices due to interest subsidies provided to customers.

Li Auto maintained its margin above 20%, however, thanks to higher-priced models accounting for a greater share of sales volume and its solid progress in autonomous-driving technology. The EV maker sold 158,696 vehicles during the period, up from the previous quarter but slightly missing its delivery guidance.

For the first quarter, the company said it expects to deliver between 88,000 and 93,000 vehicles and post revenue of 23.4 billion yuan to 24.7 billion yuan.

Known for its premium plug-in hybrids, Li Auto stands out as one of the few profitable Chinese EV makers. For 2024, its net profit fell 31% to 8.03 billion yuan and gross margin slipped to 20.5%. That compared with U.S. rival Tesla's 17.9% margin for the year. Year to date, Nasdaq-listed Li Auto has climbed 20%, while Tesla's stock has slumped 40%.

The Chinese automaker's American depositary receipts fell about 3.3% in premarket trading after the results.

Write to Jiahui Huang at jiahui.huang@wsj.com

data.title: Timee Logs 1.31 Billion Yen Profit in Q3 on Strong Labor Demand
data.description:
Timee reported a profit attributable to owners of the parent of 1.31 billion yen for the three months ended Jan. 31, according to a Tokyo bourse filing on Wednesday.

The media company's earnings per share (EPS) stood at 12.29 yen and net sales came in at 8.64 billion yen.

Comparison figures for the previous year were not provided.

For the fiscal year ending Oct. 31, Timee expects net sales to range between 34.39 billion yen and 35.70 billion yen, representing a growth of up to 32.8%.

Attributable profit is forecast to be in the range of 4.38 billion yen to 4.93 billion yen, with EPS projected between 45.12 yen and 50.78 yen.

The company does not plan to pay dividends for the fiscal year.
